@graph.name: Jaguar models fitted with the AJ-V8 4.0/4.2

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar AJ-V8 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ-V8 typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only, varying by displacement (4.0L vs 4.2L) and whether it's naturally aspirated or supercharged. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar AJ-V8 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ-V8 earns 3.5★ out of 5, based on UK enquiry data and specialist reports — a smooth, characterful V8 with genuine longevity potential, provided the timing chain tensioners have been updated.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common Jaguar AJ-V8 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Plastic timing chain tensioners on pre-2002 4.0L engines — they crack, allowing chain slack and catastrophic valve/piston contact. The 4.2L received revised, more durable tensioners.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar models use the AJ-V8?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ-V8 powered the XK8/XKR (X100), XJ8/XJR (X308/X350), S-Type/S-Type R, and early XF (X250) — eight Jaguar models in total across naturally aspirated and supercharged forms.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar AJ-V8 the same as Land Rover's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the same AJ-V8 family was also fitted to the Land Rover Range Rover L322 (4.4L variant). The core architecture is identical, though Land Rover applications use different ECU calibrations and ancillaries.

hero.h1: Jaguar AJ-V8 4.0/4.2 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar AJ-V8 is a 4.0–4.2L DOHC V8 family — smooth and characterful, but early plastic timing chain tensioners are its Achilles' heel. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025.

rows.value: Also fitted to Land Rover Range Rover/L322 → landroverengine.uk

verdictRating.confidence: 90% confidence — based on UK enquiries and cross-referenced specialist rebuild reports.

rows.score: 12 (Mix of cheap fixes (tensioners, water pump) and a few expensive ones (crankshaft/bottom-end))
16 (Well-supported but some parts (Nikasil-era blocks) now scarce)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The AJ-V8 is a smooth, characterful V8 with genuine longevity — but early plastic tensioners can destroy it without warning — buy only with proof of updated metal tensioners fitted.
verdictRating.bestFor: Enthusiasts who appreciate a refined V8 and maintain it diligently.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You cannot verify the timing chain tensioners have been updated to the metal-spec units.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers the Land Rover Range Rover L322 4.4 → landroverengine.uk

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a Jaguar AJ-V8 typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is similar across Jaguar and Land Rover applications; labour time varies by engine bay access — the XK's tight bay adds time versus the XJ's more generous layout.
